summary_one_line: A trailing zero-length IDAT chunk (4-byte 'IDAT' FOURCC, length 0, no later chunk) so the IDAT-too-many disjunction evaluates false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that reaches the chunk_name == png_IDAT arm (line 824) on a second/trailing IDAT and then drives the line-833 disjunction false: a zero-length IDAT (length==0) after the zstream ended and with PNG_HAVE_CHUNK_AFTER_IDAT unset, which requires the 4-byte chunk-name field to exactly equal the 'IDAT' FOURCC and a zero length field.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog/vpc winners resolve the branch (cmplog 9/10, vpc 8/10 resolved) and reach the false side (W reaches T=5 F=34 at line 833 vs loser T=44 F=0); I2SRandReplace can splice the logged 'IDAT' FOURCC operand and the 0 length comparand into the input bytes, manufacturing the well-formed trailing zero-length IDAT.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and value_profile losers stay blocked (naive 0/10, vp 1/10 resolved) and never take the false side (loser T=44 F=0 at line 833 — only the true 'Too many IDATs' arm fires); without I2S substitution they cannot reliably place the exact 4-byte IDAT chunk-name plus zero length to satisfy the conjunction's negation.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver buffer, finds where the chunk_name/length comparison operands appear in the input, and substitutes the matching IDAT FOURCC and zero-length values; naive and value_profile lack this mutator, so the dash-row (mutation_op = -) I2S finds in the winners' lineage are the divergence cause.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same trailing zero-length-IDAT structure reaching the line-833 false branch, but the divergence here is feedback-granularity: the false side is reached when the IDAT arm executes under a call context naive's flat edge map already considers covered.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 while naive resolves 0/10; the CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the line-833 edge reached via the trailing-IDAT path is a new (call-context, edge) bucket that rewards and retains the intermediate input, letting it be mutated toward the false side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive's flat MaxMapFeedback marks the line-833 region covered the first time any IDAT path hits it, so the trailing-zero-length-IDAT variant earns no new-coverage credit and is not retained; naive stays blocked at the branch (loser T=44 F=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook context-indexed edge map) creates new coverage buckets for the IDAT path reached under a distinct caller context, providing the retention/scheduling pressure that plain naive's edge-only feedback never supplies; no I2S or CMP_MAP is involved.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the line-833 IDAT length/flag disjunction in OSS_FUZZ_png_read_end.
The blocking condition is the disjunction over IDAT length/ZSTREAM and the chunk-after-IDAT flag.
Reaching line 833 requires the IDAT chunk-name arm at line 824.
The loser never takes the false side of the blocker while the winner reaches it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 833  T=5 F=34  T=44 F=0  || (png_ptr->mode & PNG_HAVE_CHUNK_AFTER_IDAT) != 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog wins over naive on the I2S delta, resolving 9/10 vs 0/10.
value_profile_cmplog wins over value_profile on the I2S delta.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins over naive on the ctx_coverage delta.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is a new bucket per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx feedback rewards new (call-context, edge) pairs rather than bare edges.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses no I2S or CMP_MAP, only havoc/token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3896 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 511b210a0e1497da` and observed I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 5 and 6; corroborated with `--fuzzer cmplog --seed 6b104e6c348ed6f4` showing dash rows at depth 4 and 5 (both chains share I2S ancestor 94c47bff5687a0c9).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive (no I2S, flat edge feedback) reaches the line-833 false branch at the same rate as cmplog within budget, showing the trailing zero-length-IDAT FOURCC gate is solvable by plain havoc without operand substitution or context coverage.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff offsets (0x17-0x18) sit in the IHDR length/dimension region rather than pinpointing the trailing-IDAT chunk-name bytes, so the exact input location I2S substitutes the IDAT FOURCC is inferred from source, not directly shown.
